stories.title: AI security questions loom over NATO summit
stories.summary: President Donald Trump enters the annual NATO leaders’ summit in Ankara next week with powerful leverage over the military alliance: The U.S. has the world’s most advanced artificial intelligence technology and can decide which of its allies gets access. Tech companies such as Anthropic and OpenAI have recently  announced a new wave  of sophisticated AI models capable of finding and exploiting security flaws better than most human minds. These tools can be used to  fortify c

stories.title: Will America Survive Another 250 Years? We Asked 11 Historians.
stories.summary: From a fledgling republic to a global superpower, the United States has constantly reinvented itself over the last 250 years. Americans have expanded democracy and driven innovation. They’ve also confronted war, economic upheaval and long struggles over equality and justice. Now, the country faces a new era of uncertainty: Americans are debating the United States’ role in the world, losing faith in its institutions and grappling with deep political and cultural divisions. In this moment, the sem

stories.title: ‘Attack on civil society’: why Viktor Orbán’s favourite thinktank is in crisis
stories.summary: MCC Brussels received lavish funding from Budapest, but Hungary’s new PM is launching an investigation Under glittering chandeliers in a neoclassical ballroom, guests took their seats. It was 10am and scores of people had gathered at a private members’ club in Brussels for a conference to mark 250 years of American independence , organised by Viktor Orbán’s favourite thinktank in the EU capital, MCC Brussels. Opening the one-day event, the MCC director, Frank Furedi, said the 250th anniversary h

stories.title: From ‘heat panic’ to ‘sacrificed at the altar’: Europe’s air conditioning culture wars heat up
stories.summary: Cooling down has become political amid record highs, as experts say row is distracting from work of protecting lives As the afternoon heat rose to a dizzying 41.7C (107F) in eastern Brandenburg on Sunday, taking German temperatures to unprecedented highs, Mario, 65, took precautions but did not panic. Two years ago, a fierce heatwave had prompted him to buy a powerful device that few Germans own: an air conditioning unit. “The summers are slowly getting warmer,” says the retired handyman in Neuz

stories.title: The moral test US founding father Thomas Jefferson failed?
stories.summary: Polish general and freedom fighter Thaddeus Kosciuszko presented US founding father Thomas Jefferson with a moral test. Historians still disagree on whether Jefferson passed or not.

stories.title: Hamenei ajatollah temetési szertartásán a Hezbollah, a Hamász és a jemeni húszik tisztviselői is részt vettek
stories.summary: A Hezbollah és a Hamász küldöttei Abbász Aragcsi iráni külügyminiszterrel is találkoztak a helyi média szerint.

stories.title: Más lehet az egyik leggyakoribb stroke-típus oka, mint eddig gondolták
stories.summary: Ez magyarázatot adhat arra is, miért nem hatásosak a jelenlegi megelőző kezelések.

stories.title: Egy új elmélet szerint az emberi agy nem tudja tartani a tempót a modern világgal
stories.summary: Szingapúri kutatók azt mondják: magyarázhatók lehetnek a mentális problémák azzal, hogy az agyunk egy teljesen másfajta világra fejlődött ki, mint amilyen most körülvesz minket.

stories.title: Az árakról mit mondjak? Amíg itt 1950 forint egy sajtos-tejfölös lángos, addig Siófokon 2800
stories.summary: Azt gondolná az ember, hogy a hőhullámban a Balaton partja is megtelik élettel, mégis, egyelőre csak hétvégente van mozgás a magyar tengernél. Ráadásul az erős forint sem tesz jót a Balatonnak.

stories.title: Tiniként tér vissza Doktor Szöszi
stories.summary: Eheti streamingajánlónkban Doktor Szöszi mellett helyet kapott John Cena és az idióta testvére, egy horrorbújócska és a Siló harmadik évada is.

stories.title: Two Romanians jailed over stabbing of Iranian TV journalist in London
stories.summary: Judge says evidence indicates attack on Pouria Zeraati outside home was carried out on behalf of Tehran regime Two Romanians who took part in a “targeted” knife attack on a television journalist in London “on behalf of the Iranian state” have been jailed. Pouria Zeraati, who worked for the Persian-language channel Iran International, which is critical of the Tehran regime, was left bleeding in the street after being stabbed three times outside his home in Wimbledon. Continue reading...

stories.title: Hype, pluszköltség vagy csodafegyver? Magyar cégvezetők elmondták, mit látnak az MI-ben
stories.summary: A mesterséges intelligencia remek, de azért nem árt egy kis óvatosság. A saját munka, az alapok nem nélkülözhetők, és a költségek is tetemesek – vélték a legnagyobb hazai cégek vezetői egy fórumon.

stories.title: Sulyok és Polt azonnal távozna, 12 éves időkorlát az országgyűlési képviselőknek – Magyar Péter benyújtotta az Alaptörvény 17. módosítását
stories.summary: Megszüntetik Sulyok Tamás köztársasági elnök megbízatását, 12 évre korlátozzák a képviselők mandátumát, és újra megyék lesznek a vármegyék.

stories.title: Kínai utasszállító repülőgép miatt riasztották a NATO készenléti gépeit
stories.summary: Ruszin-Szendi Romulusz szerint egy Hongkong és London között közlekedő gép még a román légtérben nem vette fel a kapcsolatot a román légiirányítással.

stories.summary: A Fidesz frakcióvezetője alkotmányos válsághelyzetként értékelte a helyzetet, és sérelmezte, hogy a jelenlegi ellenzéki képviselők több mint fele nem lesz többé választható.

stories.title: Antonelli a brit sprintfutam után az időmérőt is behúzta
stories.summary: Russell az első körben a falban kötött ki, de végül negyedik lett.

stories.title: Kanada–Marokkó 0-3
stories.summary: Bono nagy védése után Unahi bombagóllal duplázott, majd a félidő ráadásában egy újabb kontra végén Brahim Díaz második gólpasszát Szufian Rahimi gurította el higgadtan Maxime Crépeau mellett.

stories.title: Magyar Péter a Kutyapárt videóját osztotta meg Áder villájáról
stories.summary: Kovács Gergely elmesélte, hogy a négy évvel ezelőtti drónozásnál a rendőrök lefotózták a kutyapártosok drónját, később kiszabtak 50 ezer forintnyi bírságot, illetve egy NAV-vizsgálat is indult az ügyben.

stories.title: Vingegaard az első szakaszon megverte az ötödik Tour de France-győzelmére készülő Pogačart
stories.summary: A barcelonai csapatidőfutamon a legnagyobb favorit Pogačar csak harmadik lett, de a nagy riválisok közel vannak egymáshoz.

stories.title: Nagyra nőtt a szakadék Putyin szavai és a benzinért sorban álló oroszok millióinak valósága között
stories.summary: Az ukrán dróntámadások okozta benzinhiány árnyékában Putyin a magabiztosnak szánt nyilatkozataival is egyre inkább az orosz hiányosságokra világít rá mind otthon, mind a fronton. Ezt már azok is látják, akik csak az orosz állami propagandából tájékozódnak, és egyre kevésbé akarják folytatni Putyin háborúját.

stories.title: Luxus lett a szórakozás, és ez egyre nyomorúságosabbá teszi az amerikaiak életét
stories.summary: Valós jelenség a szórakozáshiány és a szabadidős programok brutális drágulása az Egyesült Államokban, mindez a személyes találkozások visszaszorulásában is szerepet játszhat.

stories.title: Marokkó elintézte Kanadát, 3-0
stories.summary: Az afrikai válogatott előbb rögzített helyzetből, majd kontrákból lett eredményes. Kanada a házigazdák közül elsőnek búcsúzik.

stories.title: Török Gábor a Fidesz parlamenti bojkottján morfondírozik, szerinte a párt helyzete jelenleg elég reménytelen
stories.summary: A politikai elemző azt írta, ha a Fidesz korlátozná vagy felfüggesztené a parlamenti munkát, azzal elég gyorsan véget vethetne Magyar Péter látványos parlamenti szerepléseinek.

stories.title: Kielce pogrom in Poland: 80th anniversary of 1946 massacre
stories.summary: Just over a year after World War II ended, a mob in the Polish city of Kielce killed around 40 Jews, some of them Holocaust survivors. A lie about ritual child murder escalated the violence.

stories.title: How many more Lyhannas must there be before France takes child sexual assault seriously? | Rokhaya Diallo
stories.summary: The outrage following the 11-year-old girl’s killing is justified. But the case reveals a deeper systemic neglect of child protection When the body of an 11-year-old girl was found in a disused grain silo on a farm in the Gers region of south-west France last month, the news sent shockwaves across the country . Lyhanna had been missing for nearly a week . Members of the public had been out combing the area. Suspicion quickly focused on Jérôme Barella, the 41-year-old father of one of Lyhanna’s c

stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Trump repositions himself as peacemaker in long call with Putin
stories.summary: Ahead of Nato summit in Turkey, Zelenskyy also tells Trump ‘there is a real prospect to end this war’. What we know on day 1,593 US president Donald Trump had a 90-minute phone call with Russian president Vladimir Putin, ⁠during which the US leader offered to help find ⁠a solution to ​the Ukraine war , a Kremlin aide has said. The aide, Yuri Ushakov, said Trump made the offer during a call with Putin on Saturday ⁠in the context of Trump’s participation next week at a Nato summit in Turkey. “The

stories.title: Coalition criticises Labor over electricity prices as Bowen rejects ‘hypocrisy’ – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed. Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Australian Space Agency has confirmed that “suspected space debris” thought to contain hazardous chemicals has been located north of Townsville. Queensland police said four objects were located on Friday and Saturday in the Forrest beach area and 50-metre exclusion zones had been enforced around each object. Continue reading...

stories.title: German riot police clash with protesters attempting to block far-right AfD conference – video
stories.summary: Riot police have clashed with opponents of the far-right Alternative für Deutschland (AfD) party on the streets of Erfurt in Germany, where thousands met to block roads and prevent AfD delegates from attending the party’s biennial national conference to elect its leadership. Police reported 20,000 protesters were demonstrating in the eastern city, where Alice Weidel and Tino Chrupalla are expected to be re-elected as the party’s co-leaders in the run-up to crucial regional elections in which AfD

stories.title: German riot police clash with protesters hoping to block far-right AfD conference
stories.summary: Thousands of police deployed to Erfurt in central Germany as party holds conference on key Nazi date Riot police have clashed with opponents of the far-right Alternative für Deutschland (AfD) party on the streets of Erfurt in Germany, where thousands met to block roads and prevent AfD delegates from attending the party’s biennial national conference to elect its leadership. Police reported 20,000 protesters were demonstrating in the eastern city, where Alice Weidel and Tino Chrupalla are expecte

stories.title: Ukrainian woman wanted after Monaco attack
stories.summary: A 39-year-old Ukrainian woman is wanted for having allegedly planted a parcel bomb in central Monaco earlier this week, gravely wounding three people. The suspect has been identified as Anastasiia Berezovska, according to an Interpol notice issued on Friday. Monaco’s head prosecutor Stéphane Thibault said on Friday that one of the three victims remained in life-threatening condition. Thibault said the suspect, whose last known address was in Germany, was specifically targeting the three ma

stories.title: NSW Labor toughens pokies stance as Sydney inner west mayor points to ‘unstoppable’ momentum for reform
stories.summary: State Labor conference passes motion committing to higher taxes on some clubs and moratorium on licenses for new machines Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The New South Wales premier, Chris Minns, will take a tougher stance on poker machines to the next election after being pushed to adopt the approach by the party’s left. A motion which passed the NSW Labor conference with unanimous support on Sunday afternoon has added a plan to take “decisive action” on problem gam

stories.title: US 250th Independence Day: Trump takes center stage
stories.summary: The US is celebrating its 250th birthday with a fireworks display and military flyovers in the capital. President Donald Trump delivers a late-night address after thunderstorms prompted an evacuation order.

stories.title: Albania PM Rama: Protests a stage for anti-Trump forces
stories.summary: Protesters in Albania say their government is selling the country its nature out to foreign investors and luxury developers. Albanian Prime Minister Edi Rama tells DW why he thinks people are so upset.

stories.title: Thousands of protesters in Germany rally against far-right AfD
stories.summary: The Alternative for Germany (AfD) party’s annual congress started Saturday in the city of Erfurt with thousands of protesters gathered against the rising German far-right movement. The demonstrators sought to blockade roads used to access the conference, local police said, though this did not prevent the event from starting on time. Video footage posted to social media showed police clashing with a group of protesters flying antifa flags. “To the troublemakers out there at the door:

stories.title: Female US rower completes historic solo journey from California to Hawaii
stories.summary: Kelsey Pfendler set out to become first US woman, youngest woman and fastest woman to solo over 2,400-mile journey A Grand Canyon river-rafting guide who aimed to become the first US woman to row solo across the mid-Pacific has completed a record-breaking journey from California to Hawaii . Hundreds of people gathered to cheer on Kelsey Pfendler as she pulled into a Honolulu harbor on Friday night on her 21ft rowboat, Lily, after nearly a month-and-a-half at sea, local media reported. Guardian s

stories.title: Cow corner: cricket match abandoned after runaway bull stops play
stories.summary: Match at Burnopfield near Newcastle called off after 800kg young bull escaped from farm and stormed playing field A cricket match had to be abandoned after a runaway bull stormed the playing field and charged at players. The North East Premier League fixture at Burnopfield Cricket Club near Newcastle was called off on Saturday evening despite efforts to remove the animal from the ground. Continue reading...

stories.title: Founder of prominent underground church released from prison in China
stories.summary: Ezra Jin, founder of Zion Church, lands in the US and reunites with family who are ‘overwhelmed with joy’ The founder of one of China’s most prominent underground churches has been released from prison and reunited with his family in the United States. Ezra Jin, the founder of Zion Church, landed in the US on Friday evening. He was one of dozens of church members who were detained in a sweeping crackdown on Christians in October . Continue reading...

stories.title: Make Ed Miliband chancellor, ex-chief Treasury adviser tells Andy Burnham
stories.summary: Nicholas Stern joins growing number backing Miliband, saying he has vision and experience to revive economy A former chief economic adviser to the Treasury has called on Andy Burnham to appoint Ed Miliband as chancellor, arguing the energy secretary has a “bold” vision to revive the economy. Nicholas Stern, a professor at the London School of Economics who was a senior figure in the Treasury during Gordon Brown’s tenure, said only Miliband had the experience and the strategic vision to accelerat

stories.title: Liberal frontbencher Dan Tehan says coalition with One Nation ‘not even being talked about’
stories.summary: Anthony Albanese mocks ructions on conservative side of politics and says three rightwing parties are caught up in ‘race to the bottom’ Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Liberal frontbencher Dan Tehan says he does not want to be in a rightwing coalition with One Nation, despite senior colleagues urging closer ties with Pauline Hanson and polls suggesting her rise will make it difficult for the opposition party to ever govern in its own right again. The prime minister,

stories.title: Mysterious debris found on Queensland beaches could be ‘space balls’ – and may contain toxic rocket fuel
stories.summary: Australian Space Agency working to confirm origin of objects as space archaeologist says they may be connected to a rocket launch Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Six pieces of suspected space debris found washed up on north Queensland beaches could be “space balls” that are often left over from rocket launches, according to one expert. The Australian Space Agency confirmed on Sunday it was working to determine the nature and origin of the mysterious objects, which po

stories.title: Woman charged with murder after body of four-year-old boy found in Central Coast home
stories.summary: Police are investigating what happened to a boy who was found with significant arm injuries Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A woman has been charged with murder after police found the body of a preschooler at a home on the New South Wales Central Coast. Police rushed to the home at Wyong after a 32-year-old woman presented to the local police station on Saturday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Baptism record at Manchester Cathedral offers insight into Black Mancunian life in Georgian-era England
stories.summary: A parish entry reveals an argument that proved pivotal to the abolitionist cause, at a time when an estimated 20,000 Black people were living in the country When the abolitionist Thomas Clarkson gave a sermon in 1787 at Manchester Cathedral – during the city’s first mass meeting against the transatlantic trade in enslaved Africans – he saw a “great crowd of black people standing round the pulpit”. However, little is known about Black Mancunians in the Georgian era, which makes one recently redis

stories.title: UK woman, 21, arrested in Thailand after allegedly stabbing boyfriend to death
stories.summary: Officers found body of 34-year-old man in luxury rental home in Pattaya area, local media says A 21-year-old British woman has been arrested in Thailand after allegedly fatally stabbing her boyfriend, according to local media reports. The Bangkok Post reported that on Thursday morning local time, officers found the body of a 34-year-old man, who operated a cannabis farm, in a luxury rental home in the Pattaya area, a beachside region two hours from Bangkok known for its large expat population an

stories.title: ‘The situation is terrible’: aid workers on life in Sudanese city pummelled by drone strikes
stories.summary: El Obeid becomes key battleground in war between Sudan’s armed forces and their paramilitary enemies, the RSF Fatima has lost count of the number of drone attacks on the besieged city of El Obeid in Sudan, but said the attacks this past weekend were the most violent so far. The drones hit schools and fuel stations, killing more than 20 people, including students, she said. “Over the past few months, seeing 40 or 45 drones is the norm. You can literally count them,” said the aid volunteer, whose

stories.title: Ukrainian drones hit St Petersburg oil terminal and nearby port
stories.summary: St Petersburg governor reports no victims after ‘large-scale’ overnight attack that also hit Baltic port of Vysotsk Ukraine launched a big overnight drone attack on St Petersburg and the surrounding area, hitting the city’s oil terminal and port infrastructure in the wider region. The St Petersburg governor, Alexander Beglov, said the city had been subjected to a “large-scale” drone attack that had hit its oil terminal. He said there were no casualties and the aftermath of the attack had been de

stories.title: Neo-fascist group Patriot Front parades Confederate flag in Washington DC on Fourth of July
stories.summary: Hundreds of masked members of the white supremacist organization marched and chanted in the US capital Hundreds of masked men carrying banners, including the Confederate flag, marched through Washington DC on the Fourth of July, the 250th anniversary of the US’s inception. The group appeared to be led by Thomas Rousseau, founder of the neo-fascist, white supremacist organization Patriot Front. Members of the group wore white masks and gathered in front of DC’s Union Station. They later marched t

stories.title: Venezuela earthquake death toll nears 3,000 as hope to find survivors dims
stories.summary: International rescue teams have begun closing up operations as deaths in Venezuela double quakes approach 3,000. Families are still looking for signs of their missing loved ones.

stories.title: Jehovah's Witnesses fight German state for Holocaust archive
stories.summary: The Jehovah's Witnesses have secured a partial victory in a dispute over a unique archive documenting the community's persecution by the Nazi regime. It comes after a new memorial to the victims was unveiled in Berlin.

stories.title: Trump set to put NATO unity to the test at Turkey summit
stories.summary: NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte is working to keep the alliance united and Donald Trump engaged. But disputes over burden sharing, US military commitments, and the Iran war are creating a major test for NATO unity.

stories.title: Welcoming the Taliban? — Germany's new Afghanistan policy
stories.summary: To facilitate the deportation of criminals to Afghanistan, the German government is allowing Taliban diplomats into the country.

stories.title: Ukraine strikes oil terminals near St. Petersburg in Russia
stories.summary: The long-range strikes are the latest salvo in Ukraine’s expanding campaign to inflict economic damage on Russia and hinder its war machine.

stories.title: How Trump took over America’s 250th
stories.summary: America turns 250 on Saturday. But the celebration Americans will see isn’t quite the one organizers spent the last decade planning. For nearly 10 years, the bipartisan, congressionally chartered America250 envisioned a once-in-a-generation civic commemoration built around history, service and local communities. One America250 planning document imagined transforming the tiny Badlands town of Medora, North Dakota — population roughly 150 — into a living museum recreating Theodore Roosevelt’s

stories.title: Funeral of Iran's former supreme leader 'intensely political moment'
stories.summary: Authorities expect up to 20 million people to attend ceremonies across Iran and Iraq over the coming days.

stories.title: Moment of destiny for France's Le Pen in verdict to decide her future in presidential race
stories.summary: Far-right leader Marine Le Pen is appealing against a verdict which found her guilty of misusing EU funds.

stories.title: Chinese underground church figure Jin Mingri freed from prison
stories.summary: The Zion Church founder's release comes after a direct plea to Xi Jinping from Donald Trump.

stories.title: Fireworks light up Washington sky for America's 250th anniversary
stories.summary: A fireworks display illuminated the National Mall in Washington as the United States marked the 250th anniversary of its independence.

stories.title: 'The American Dream is back,' Trump says during Fourth of July speech
stories.summary: "Our warriors did not fight communism on battlefields across the world only to have that menace rear its ugly head right back here in America," Trump told a cheering crowd.

stories.title: Scorching heat and delays: How Washington DC marked America 250
stories.summary: The United States of America marked its 250th birthday with fireworks, flyovers, some intense weather across the country.

stories.title: US marks 250th birthday with fireworks, flyovers and extreme weather
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump's speech included some of his political agenda but also honoured war veterans and American history.

stories.title: Zelenskyy and Putin hold Fourth of July phone calls with Trump
stories.summary: Yuri Ushakov, a Russian presidential aide, said Putin and Trump discussed "the issue of the Ukrainian settlement" in a phone call that lasted an hour and twenty-five minutes.

stories.title: Ukraine hits major oil terminal in Russia's St Petersburg
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky says the target is key infrastructure "that generates revenue for Russia's war".

stories.title: Celtic nations begin to plan for breakup of UK in event of Reform election win
stories.summary: Politicians brace for constitutional turmoil if Nigel Farage’s party end up in government – or even as a strong opposition The rise of Nigel Farage has prompted political leaders across Ireland, Northern Ireland, Scotland and Wales to game the unthinkable: the breakup of the United Kingdom. Unionists who wish to save the union and nationalists who wish to end it are bracing for constitutional turmoil if Reform UK emerges triumphant – with Farage as prime minister or official leader of the opposi

stories.title: Pope praises US history of welcoming migrants in implicit rebuke to Trump
stories.summary: Pope Leo urges Americans to live up to ideals of Declaration of Independence in first key address to US Pope Leo has used his first key address to his home country to praise the US history of ⁠welcoming migrants, urging Americans to live up to the ideals put forward in the ⁠Declaration of Independence. In his latest implicit rebuke to Donald Trump, the first US leader of the Roman Catholic church said the word “America” had become ‌a “byword for freedom” across the world because of the way the c

stories.title: How the 1986 Mexico World Cup was almost cancelled after a devastating earthquake
stories.summary: Guardian reports after the disaster told of 5,000 deaths, much of the capital being razed, and doubts about Mexico hosting the finals Mexico last hosted the World Cup in 1986 , but the competition was almost cancelled several months before the start when an earthquake struck the capital, Mexico City, leaving at least 5,000 people dead, 30,000 homeless and much of the city flattened, in one of the worst earthquakes to hit the country. To this day, the death toll remains disputed, with some estima

stories.title: Crowds gather as six-day funeral for former Iranian supreme leader begins
stories.summary: Up to 30 million people expected to attend delayed events for Ali Khamenei, killed at start of war with US and Israel Huge crowds have gathered at the funeral of the former Iranian supreme leader after the gates of the sprawling Grand Mosalla mosque in central Tehran let in thousands of mourners who had been waiting through the night to enter the grounds. Iran is staging mass funeral processions for Ali Khamenei – whose ⁠37-year reign was brought ⁠to an ​end in February by the first airstrike of

stories.title: Trump issues 11 pardons on eve of country’s Fourth of July celebrations
stories.summary: Pardons issued to nine people charged with violating Clean Air Act as extreme heat smothers much of US Donald Trump on Friday issued pardons to 11 men – two convicted fraudsters and nine charged with having violated the federal Clean Air Act by disabling or otherwise modifying trucks’ emissions controls. Those executive pardons – coming amid US semiquincentennial celebrations blanketed in extreme heat exacerbated by greenhouse gas emissions – were among a broader wave of acts of clemency from Tr

stories.title: Three children dead after boat capsizes during storm in Wisconsin
stories.summary: Officials rescued seven other people after a sudden storm led to a boat sinking on Geneva Lake Three children died after a boat capsized on Wisconsin ’s Geneva Lake during inclement weather on the eve of the US’s semiquincentennial celebrations, and seven other people had to be rescued by emergency responders, according to officials. A recreational motor boat with 10 passengers, including four children, sank on Friday afternoon as the boat “attempted to navigate to safety as weather conditions d

stories.title: Aerial footage shows wildfire raging across Catalonia – video
stories.summary: Aerial footage released by Catalan firefighters shows a huge plume of smoke rising from a wildfire burning in La Bisbal d’Empordà area of northeastern Spain. The wildfire has burned about 750 hectares (1,853 acres), according to provisional estimates from the Catalan rural agents service Weather tracker: Heatwave breaks June temperature records across Europe Continue reading...

stories.title: In a pickle: US pickle company pulls out of DC fair over Confederate flag
stories.summary: Mt Olive Pickle Company says it was unaware image of flag was included in exhibit, and cites value of ‘human dignity’ A leading vendor of the US delicacy that is the pickle has withdrawn from the Great American State Fair in Washington DC after North Carolina’s booth displayed a video containing a Confederate flag. The Mt Olive Pickle Company, which is located in eastern North Carolina and bills itself as the “#1 bestselling brand of pickles, peppers and relishes in the US”, told local news stat

stories.title: Archaeologists uncover ancient Byzantine city in Egypt’s western desert
stories.summary: Well-preserved fourth-century quarters reveal details of daily life, urban development and economic activities Archaeologists in Egypt have uncovered a well-preserved Byzantine-era city in the western desert. The fourth-century quarters had residential and religious structures, including a basilica-style church in the Dakhla oasis. Archaeologists also found coins, pottery fragments and tools. Continue reading...

stories.title: India news: Government lists 23 Pakistanis as 'terrorists'
stories.summary: Indian government has designated 23 Pakistan-based individuals as "terrorists" under its anti-terror law. Follow DW for more.

stories.title: Pope Leo calls on Europe to do more for migrants
stories.summary: The pontiff's trip to the migration frontline of Lampedusa underscored a stark message for Europe and the United States as intolerance and indifference rise.

stories.title: Germany news: AfD elects leaders amid massive protests
stories.summary: Party delegates of the far-right AfD are meeting in Erfurt as tens of thousands of people are protesting the event. Meanwhile, Jürgen Klopp said he was "ready" to jump in as Germany's head coach. DW has more.

stories.title: Mali rebels target army bases, prison in fresh attacks
stories.summary: The army claimed it had regained control after the coordinated assaults on several towns and cities by Tuareg rebels and Islamists.

stories.title: Cape Verde fans stand proud despite Argentina defeat
stories.summary: Cape Verde supporters expressed pride in their team's historic World Cup campaign despite a 3-2 extra-time defeat to defending champions Argentina.

stories.title: Iranians mourn as Khamenei funeral ceremonies begin
stories.summary: Large crowds gathered in Tehran as Iran began six days of funeral ceremonies for late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, who was killed during the US-Israeli conflict.

stories.title: Large crowds gather in Tehran on first day of Ayatollah Khamenei's funeral
stories.summary: Ali Khamenei's body will lie in state in Tehran's Grand Mosalla until Monday, before travelling across Iran and Iraq.

stories.title: Trump warns of communist threat as Washington parade cancelled due to heatwave
stories.summary: "Communism is a mortal threat to American liberty," Trump said during a speech on Friday. "It is the greatest threat to our country".

stories.title: Pope urges Europe to do more for migrants as he visits gateway island
stories.summary: The pontiff pays tribute to migrants who lost their lives at sea at a cemetery on the Italian island of Lampedusa.

stories.title: World leaders and royals celebrate the US's 250th birthday
stories.summary: "The story of the relationship between our two nations is one of extraordinary evolution, from overcoming conflict to forging one of the closest and most productive alliances the world has seen," King Charles III said.

stories.title: Former chess champion Kramnik banned over cheating remarks
stories.summary: The former Russian world chess champion had accused other players, including the late Daniel Naroditsky, of cheating.

stories.title: BBC in Tehran as mourners gather for former supreme leader's funeral
stories.summary: Iranian authorities say they expect millions of people to take part in funeral commemorations for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

stories.title: Injury ends Williams' Wimbledon comeback
stories.summary: Serena Williams pulls out of her planned Wimbledon doubles appearance with older sister Venus because of a knee injury.

stories.title: Dior dress, Adam Sandler and a man of honour: What we know about Taylor and Travis's wedding
stories.summary: The couple tied the knot in New York on Friday, after months of speculation about when, where and how it would happen.

stories.title: Eala wins 'for all the girls with ruffled socks and chubby cheeks'
stories.summary: Alexandra Eala dedicates her stunning win over defending Wimbledon champion Iga Swiatek to "all the girls with ruffled socks and chubby cheeks".

stories.summary: Mbappé tizenegyesével 1-0-ra nyert Franciaország a futball összes legitim és nem legitim eszközét bemutató paraguayiakkal szemben.

stories.summary: A paraguayi játékosok minden alattomos húzást bevetettek a franciák ellen a világbajnokság nyolcaddöntőjében.

stories.summary: Kik és miért dezertáltak az ukrán hadseregből? A Deutsche Welle riportjából kiderül.

stories.summary: Az Atomic Energy Lab az atomenergia megismerése irányába szerette volna terelni a gyerekeket, ezért egy sor radioaktív anyagot adott a kezükbe. A játék biztonságosnak hirdette magát, de csak addig, míg a gyerekek nem nyitották ki az uránt rejtő fiolákat.

stories.title: Kanada edzője a 3-0-s vereség után: Teljesen kontrolláltuk a mérkőzést, mi voltunk a jobbak
stories.summary: Jesse Marsch szerint a világ legjobb csapatai ellen is jobbak tudnak lenni egy adott napon.

stories.summary: Ez még csak Amerika aranykorának hajnala, mondta az amerikai elnök az ország megszületésének 250. évfordulóját ünneplő július 4-ei beszédében.

stories.title: 180 törvényben kell átírni a vármegyét megyére, a főispánt kormánymegbízottra
stories.summary: Ez 557 millió forintba kerül a kormány társadalmi egyeztetésre bocsátott tervezete szerint.

stories.summary: A francia edző a világbajnokságon vette át a szétesett csapat irányítását, ő sem tudott velük csodát tenni.

stories.summary: Putyin az oroszok Ukrajna által vitatott harctéri eredményeit ismertette, Zelenszkij a háború befejezéséről beszélt.

stories.summary: Csak egy évről van szó, jövő augusztusban ugyanis megszűnnek az egyetemeket fenntartó alapítványok is.

stories.summary: Az egykori világklasszis csatár azt mondta, nem szereti, ha provokálják, és biztosan nem tudta volna magát visszafogni egy ilyen mérkőzésen.

stories.title: ‘I can only describe it as a war zone’: the rescuers navigating Venezuela’s post-quake hellscape
stories.summary: Thousands of volunteers are joined by overseas teams in the hope of finding more survivors in the rubble, reports Tom Phillips in Caraballeda. Photography and video by Manu Quintero When twin earthquakes tore through Venezuela’s northern coast last week, Israel Rivas was at home hundreds of miles away in the industrial city of San Félix. As the scale of the catastrophe became clear, the 24-year-old knew he had to react. A mechanic and budding photographer, Rivas gathered the money he had been sa

stories.title: Wildfires rage in parts of France and Spain after record heatwave – video
stories.summary: Numerous wildfires are raging across parts of France and Spain, after record temperatures in Europe caused dry conditions and a worsening drought. Two thousand firefighters battled several wildfires fanned by strong winds along France's Mediterranean coast on Thursday, while Spain’s military emergency unit (UME) said it had deployed more than 100 personnel and about 40 vehicles to support firefighting efforts in the Aragon region Weather tracker: Heatwave breaks June temperature records across E

stories.title: Ali Khamenei’s six-day funeral expected to draw millions in Iran
stories.summary: Country’s leadership vows to never surrender as memorial on grand scale aims to relay message of resistance to world In the small hours of Friday the police roadblocks, stalls, posters and army vans were starting to appear across Tehran, as millions of Iranians prepared to attend the long-delayed six-day funeral ceremony for Ali Khamenei, Iran’s supreme leader for 36 turbulent years. Khamenei was killed aged 86 in the opening salvo of the US-Israeli attack on the country in February, and the fin

stories.title: New pipeline in Canada to proceed after C$150bn pledged to ease BC and First Nations concerns
stories.summary: Port expansion and protections for whales part of BC and Alberta plan to expand country’s presence overseas The governments of Canada and the province of Alberta will move forward on a major new oil pipeline after the pair announced a plan to ease concerns of British Columbia and First Nations on the Pacific coast. Canada’s prime minister, Mark Carney, shuttled between British Columbia and Alberta on Thursday to announce more than C$150bn in new investments in both provinces, part of a broader p

stories.title: Overseas education project for women and girls axed by UK after two years
stories.summary: The programme, aimed at keeping 1m girls in school across Africa, Asia and the Middle East, withdrawn after aid cuts A leading higher education programme, aimed at keeping 1 million girls in school across Africa, Asia and the Middle East, has been axed by the British government just two years after it was announced. The scheme, Strengthening higher education for female empowerment (SHEFE), which was unveiled with some fanfare two years ago by the outgoing Conservative government, had a £45m budg

stories.title: Az MTVA egyik meg nem erősített vezetője bocsánatot kér Pikó Andrástól, amiért 13 évvel ezelőtt bajnaizta a 17 éves lányát
stories.summary: Hivatalosan még ki sem nevezték Bodacz Balázst, már számon is kérték rajta egy korábbi Hír TV-s riportját.

stories.title: Far-right AfD threatens eastern Germany's energy transition
stories.summary: Germany's far-right AfD wants to revive coal and nuclear power and curb non-EU immigration. Businesses in eastern Germany warn that its policies could harm the economy.

stories.title: Venezuela's interim leader defends earthquake response as death toll tops 2,500
stories.summary: Delcy Rodríguez has hit back at criticism over her government’s response to the quakes, rejecting accusations of slow rescue efforts and claims that official casualty figures have been under-reported.

stories.title: Iran FM confirms reports Israel planned to target Tehran's negotiators
stories.summary: Iran's foreign minister corroborated US media reports that Washington warned Tehran Israel may have had its chief negotiators on a target list during April talks in Islamabad, the same talks that produced the framework deal signed on 17 June.

stories.title: Did he say 'Bismillah'? Cristiano Ronaldo’s penalty whisper ignites global debate
stories.summary: Superstar striker Cristiano Ronaldo’s whisper broadcast live around the world before the decisive penalty against Croatia sparked a global debate over whether it was an Arabic-inspired sacred invocation or his own pre-Saudi encouragement routine in Portuguese.

stories.title: Conservative candidate Keiko Fujimori wins Peru's presidential election in runoff
stories.summary: Voters were primarily concerned with increasing levels of crime, especially extortion by violent organized crime gangs, and Fujimori pledged to combat crime with an iron fist.

stories.title: Turkish comedian arrested for 'insulting' Islam and Erdogan
stories.summary: Turkish comedian Deniz Göktaş was arrested at Istanbul Airport on Thursday following a stand-up show posted to YouTube. The 32-year-old faces multiple probes, including "publicly insulting religious values" and insulting President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan.

stories.title: Weather tracker: Heatwave breaks June temperature records across Europe
stories.summary: UK provisional peak of 37.7C shatters previous record by huge margin, while Germany hits all-time high of 41.7C Last week’s heatwave across western Europe shattered national June records and set new all-time highs. The UK recorded a provisional high of 37.7C at Lingwood in Norfolk on Friday 27 June, smashing the previous June record of 35.6C, set in 1976. Such a margin is exceptionally rare: temperature records are typically broken by 0.1C or 0.2C, not a remarkable 2.1C. Continue reading...

stories.summary: Anasztaszija Berezovszka ukrán állampolgár, feltehetőleg Németországba menekült.

stories.summary: Szombaton ünneplik az Egyesült Államok 250. születésnapját, de az időjárás közbeszólt.

stories.title: Hőkupola borult Philadelphiára, 43 fok is lehet a franciák Paraguay elleni nyolcaddöntőjén a pályán
stories.summary: Az egy dolog, hogy végre lesz értelme az ivószünetnek, de milyen extra terhelést jelent az ilyen hőség a játékosoknak?

stories.title: Pár év múlva tömegével jöhetnek a humanoid robotok a munkahelyekre
stories.summary: Az üzletileg is hasznos feladatok sikeraránya elérte a 95 százalékot, és egy-két éven belül elérhető a 99 százalékos küszöbérték, ez a szint pedig már lehetővé tenné a gazdaságos üzleti alkalmazást.

stories.title: Serena Williams sérülés miatt visszalép a wimbledoni teniszversenytől
stories.summary: Testvérével indult volna párosban, de egy korábbi mérkőzésén elszenvedett térdsérülése miatt kénytelen volt visszalépni.

stories.title: Ez a romantikus film többet tanít a gyászról, mint a szerelemről
stories.summary: Az Üzenet Isabelle-nek tökéletesen megmutatja, hogy továbbra is óriási igény lenne igazán jó romantikus filmekre, kár, hogy a készítők nem bíztak jobban a saját történetükben. Kritika.

stories.title: Szülőországa ellen játszik a marokkói kapus a nyolcaddöntőben
stories.summary: Jászín Bono Montrealban született, és három éves volt, mikor családjával együtt Casablancába költöztek.

stories.title: Harry herceg egyedül utazik Londonba, mert családja nem kap rendőri védelmet
stories.summary: Hivatalosan is kérvényezte, hogy rendőrségi védelmet kapjanak az utazás alatt, de közölték velük, hogy azt nem kapják meg.

stories.title: Gyógyultan hagyta el a kórházat az első franciaországi ebolás beteg
stories.summary: 2014-ben, egy jelentős nyugat-afrikai járvány idején két pácienst ápoltak Franciaországban, de náluk külföldön diagnosztizálták a betegséget.

stories.title: Hazát cserélni nagy rizikó, és mindig meg kell fizetni az árát
stories.summary: Mit nyer és mit veszít az ember akkor, amikor az emigrációt választja? A Teheránban született, Los Angelesben felnőtt Sanam Mahloudji regényének egyik központi kérdése a menni vagy maradni dilemmája. Kritika.

stories.summary: Az AfD kongresszusát igyekeztek megakadályozni Erfurt városában, ahol legalább harmincezren demonstráltak a szélsőjobboldali párt ellen.

stories.summary: Perceken belül öt és nyolc Celsius-fokkal csökkenti a hőmérsékletet.

stories.title: Sick leave: Germany rising but not the worst in Europe
stories.summary: Sick leave in Germany has hit a new record, with workers taking an average of nearly 20 days off per year. As Chancellor Merz cracks down, how does the country's absenteeism compare to other countries?

stories.title: Free electricity in Australia, and the week's other eco wins
stories.summary: Germans save an ancient woodland from coal mining, Australia gives away surplus solar power, and how one city became the world's best place for cyclists?

stories.title: Maltese tycoon faces trial over Caruana Galizia murder
stories.summary: The Maltese businessman accused of masterminding journalist Daphne Caruana Galizia’s 2017 murder is in court — seven years after his arrest. Five other men have already been found guilty for their roles in the murder.

stories.title: Lyra McKee: 3 men acquitted of killing N. Irish journalist
stories.summary: McKee was killed while covering clashes between Republicans and police in 2019. Judge Patricia Smyth said evidence was insufficient for a conviction, while McKee's family expressed disappointment.

stories.title: NASA launches spacecraft to recover sinking telescope
stories.summary: The Swift telescope has been affected by recent solar storms and is sinking faster than ever, heading toward Earth. A NASA mission will try to capture it and boost its orbit.

stories.title: Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce tie knot in star-studded ceremony
stories.summary: The couple got married in a major star-studded celebration at Madison Square Garden over the Fourth of July weekend.

stories.title: Heat wave upends July Fourth celebrations across US
stories.summary: A dangerous heat wave has disrupted July Fourth events across the United States, including Donald Trump's flagship Great American State Fair, as the nation prepares to mark its 250th birthday.

stories.title: Iran: Khamenei's funeral ceremonies officially begin
stories.summary: Huge crowds have gathered in Tehran to pay their final respects to Iran's supreme leader, who was killed in an airstrike at the start of the US-Israeli campaign against Iran.

stories.title: US: President Trump pardons 11 people, including Clean Air Act violators
stories.summary: Donald Trump has granted pardons to nine people convicted of violating a key environmental law. The US President has also pardoned an associate of convicted felon and former lobbyist Jack Abramoff.

stories.title: Australia's Great Barrier Reef again avoids 'in danger' label
stories.summary: UNESCO acknowledged Australia's efforts towards the Great Barrier Reef's health but raised concerns over mass bleaching. For now, the popular marine site seems to have dodged the "endangered" tag.

stories.title: Rescued bile bears released in Denmark forest
stories.summary: Six rescued Asiatic black bears from South Korea start a new life in Denmark’s Knuthenborg Safaripark after an international rescue operation.

stories.title: Dogs don Argentina shirts as World Cup fever grips Buenos Aires
stories.summary: A dog walker in Buenos Aires leads dogs dressed in Argentina jerseys through the city as World Cup fever grows and fans celebrate in the streets.

stories.title: France records 2,025 excess deaths at peak of heatwave as Europe braces for more extreme weather
stories.summary: Forecasters are warning of further extreme temperatures on the continent in the next few days.

stories.title: Iran to host dozens of foreign leaders for Khamenei's funeral, with Western nations absent
stories.summary: Russia's Medvedev, Pakistan's prime minister and Taliban officials are among roughly 30 country delegations expected as Iran buries slain Ayatollah Ali Khamenei this week.

stories.title: Video shows moment 11-year-old drives pickup truck into Buddhist procession
stories.summary: Ten people were killed after an 11-year-old boy drove a pickup truck into a Buddhist procession in northeastern Thailand.

stories.title: Overcrowded bus plunges into ravine in southwest Pakistan, killing 40 passengers
stories.summary: Pakistan’s President Asif Ali Zardari expressed sorrow over the crash, offered condolences to the families of those killed and wished the injured a speedy recovery.

stories.title: Ukrainian suspect hunted by police after Monaco bomb attack was 'disguised as a man'
stories.summary: Officials believe the suspect may not have acted alone and spent days casing out the scene of the attack.

stories.title: Mourners in Damascus bury cafe bomb blast victims as officials promise justice
stories.summary: Although there is so far no indication who carried out the attack, many were quick to blame loyalists of former President Bashar al-Assad, who was ousted in an insurgent offensive in December 2024.

stories.title: EBRD backs Kazakhstan's move up the minerals value chain with €255m loan
stories.summary: The European Bank for Reconstruction and Development (EBRD) is financing Kazakhstan's first large-scale refractory gold ore processing facility, helping the country unlock previously inaccessible reserves while expanding domestic mineral processing.

stories.title: Anguished families left to identify Venezuela quake victims at makeshift morgue
stories.summary: The disaster has overwhelmed local services, with bodies put outside or in tents for identification.

stories.title: More cows than pupils - what is behind mass school closures in rural Kenya?
stories.summary: More than 2,000 primary schools are facing closure as enrolment numbers plummet.

stories.title: Row after Indian state drops eggs from school lunch menu
stories.summary: West Bengal's move to replace eggs in some school meals has sparked a wider debate over nutrition and choice.

stories.summary: Temperature records were smashed in June - and scientists warn this is a sign of things to come.

stories.summary: The star-studded celebration saw several streets in Midtown Manhattan shut, as hordes of fans gathered outside the arena.

stories.summary: The couple tied the knot in a star-studded ceremony officiated by comedian Adam Sandler at Madison Square Garden.

stories.title: 'Vanishingly rare' copy of US Declaration of Independence discovered in the UK
stories.summary: Graham Moore, the Revolution 250 curator at the National Archives, said the "exceptional" item was "one of the rarest forms of the Declaration we know about".

stories.title: Keiko Fujimori declared winner of Peru's presidential election weeks after vote
stories.summary: The daughter of disgraced president Alberto Fujimori is declared winner of tight race nearly a month after voting took place.

stories.title: Côte d’Ivoire floods kill 59 as west Africa endures torrential rains
stories.summary: Authorities say rainy season getting deadlier, with Ghana reporting 13 dead and floods hitting Benin, Togo and Nigeria Floods in Côte d’Ivoire have killed 59 people since May, the communication minister told a cabinet meeting in Abidjan. There are fears the toll could further rise as rescue teams continue to search for victims during the rainy season, which runs from May until July, the minister, Amadou Coulibaly, added. Continue reading...

stories.title: Papua separatists kill American pilot in ‘message’ to US and Indonesia
stories.summary: Rebels shoot pilot and set his civilian ⁠plane on fire amid long-running low-level battle for independence in region Separatist rebels in Indonesia’s restive easternmost region of Papua have shot dead an American pilot and set a civilian ⁠plane on fire, in what a spokesperson for a local militant group described as a “message” to the US and Indonesian governments. Sebby Sambom, ⁠a spokesperson for the West Papua National Liberation Army (TPNPB), named the pilot as Nicholas F Gosselin and said se

stories.title: Turkish comedian Deniz Goktas detained at Istanbul airport
stories.summary: Turkish stand-up comedian Deniz Goktas was detained on his return to Turkey, days after a video he posted online gained traction. The clip included references to President Recep Tayyip Erdogan and religion.

stories.title: Smoke rises over Kyiv after Russian overnight strikes
stories.summary: Smoke rises over Kyiv after a large-scale Russian missile and drone attack overnight that killed at least eight people and injured dozens.

stories.title: Von der Leyen unveils major EU support initiatives in strategic trip to Armenia
stories.summary: European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen announced a significant economic and political support package to Armenia during her strategic visit to engage with the South Caucasus peace process and the new opportunities in the region formerly in Russia’s orbit.

stories.title: Air conditioner rush: Scuffles break out at Lidl stores across France
stories.summary: Hundreds of shoppers queued outside Lidl supermarkets across France on Thursday after the retailer announced the sale of 200,000 fans and portable air conditioners, triggering long lines, scuffles and police intervention at several stores.

stories.title: At least nine monks killed in Thailand after boy drives truck into procession
stories.summary: Charges yet to be filed over incident in Mukdahan involving 11-year-old, as police seek to establish circumstances An 11-year-old boy has driven his parents’ truck into a Buddhist procession in Thailand, killing at least nine monks. CCTV footage shared by a local rescue group showed the moment the monks, wearing orange robes, were run over as they walked in procession along a road. The timestamp on the footage was shortly before 11am local time on Thursday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Canadian boy dies of rabies after waking to find bat on his face
stories.summary: Eleven-year-old developed symptoms 19 days after encounter in Ontario in ‘exceedingly rare’ case Doctors in Canada say a child who awoke to find a bat resting on his nose and mouth while visiting an Ontario cottage later died of rabies, in an “exceedingly rare case” that highlights the need for better public awareness. In a report published this week in the Canadian Medical Association Journal, infectious disease physicians confirmed that the 11-year-old boy died from rabies, a fatality they sai

stories.title: Venezuelan man saved from collapsed mall eight days after earthquakes
stories.summary: Security guard Hernán Alberto Gil Flores, 43, initially told rescuers not to tell his wife in case he did not survive A 43-year-old security guard who survived last week’s devastating earthquakes in Venezuela thanks to a pocket of air in his workstation cabin has been pulled from the collapsed basement of a shopping centre amid huge cheers from international rescue teams. Hernán Alberto Gil Flores had been trapped for eight days under the rubble of the Galerías Playa Grande in the hard-hit coast

stories.title: ‘Give him any award, and he’ll come running’: Narendra Modi racks up honours on overseas trips
stories.summary: Indian prime minister has a habit of collecting awards on his travels, some as their first and only recipient As Narendra Modi touched down in Seychelles over the weekend, the archipelago nation in the Indian Ocean swiftly bestowed one of its “highest” honours upon the Indian prime minister. Modi beamed as he accepted the Guardian of the Blue Horizon award from Patrick Herminie, the Seychelles president, complete with a trophy and certificate. Continue reading...

stories.title: Race to control wildfires in southern France – in pictures

The fire started at a campsite, destroying dozens of mobile homes before spreading to the marina area, where thick, toxic smoke blanketed boats. The fire broke out in the town of Sainte-Marie-la-Mer and spread to Canet-en-Roussillon on Thursday [• This gallery’s headline and subheading were amended on 3 July 2026; an earlier version referred to south-western France ] Continue re

stories.title: China says man who flew plane into Beijing skyscraper had mental health problems
stories.summary: Official statement offers the most detailed official account yet of the highly unusual and fatal incident in Beijing Chinese authorities said the man who flew a small plane into Beijing’s tallest skyscraper last week was a 66-year-old who had mental health problems. The statement published on Thursday offered the most detailed official account yet of the highly unusual incident that occurred in Beijing’s central business district on the evening of 26 June. Continue reading...

stories.title: Fact check: Racist German fan petition story false
stories.summary: It has been claimed that following Germany's World Cup exit, some fans launched a petition seeking to ban "African" and "Muslim" players from the national team — and that DW reported the story. This is not true.

stories.title: Vatican excommunicates rebel SSPX bishops, followers
stories.summary: The Vatican's watchdog authority has imposed severe disciplinary measures on an ultratraditionalist Catholic group. The move comes after the group, the Society of Saint Pius X, consecrated bishops without papal consent.

stories.title: Is Mexico the 2026 World Cup's best host country?
stories.summary: Mexico has fully embraced its role as a World Cup host. Visitors are raving about the atmosphere and say they wish that there were more knockout-stage matches in Mexico as opposed to in the United States.

stories.title: German public university creates Islamic theology faculty
stories.summary: The University of Münster is the first public university in Europe to establish an Islamic theology faculty, a move that is is attracting international attention.

stories.title: German coalition agrees on changes to pensions, tax rates
stories.summary: After its first year in office, Germany's coalition government plans to push through sweeping changes with the stated goal of reviving a sluggish economy. The plans met with a mixed reaction.

stories.title: AI drones made in Germany see duty on Ukraine's front line
stories.summary: The German defense company Helsing is supplying combat drones to Ukraine and will soon provide them to the German army. How effective are they on the front line? DW joined a Ukrainian combat mission to find out.

stories.title: As heat waves loom, scientists wonder how humans will adapt
stories.summary: Temperatures are set to soar in the US around the July 4 holiday, a week after Europe struck new records. With experts predicting more intense and more frequent heat waves in the future, can our bodies adapt?

stories.title: US fans celebrate World Cup last-16 spot
stories.summary: US supporters celebrate in Carson, California, after the United States beat Bosnia-Herzegovina 2-0 to reach the World Cup round of 16.

stories.title: Kazakhstan starts living by new Constitution, sets date for parliamentary elections
stories.summary: Kazakhstan's new Constitution entered into force on July 1, prompting the Kazakh President to set August 23 as the date for parliamentary elections required under the new constitutional framework.

stories.title: Fast-moving wildfire spreads across southern France
stories.summary: Firefighters battle a fast-moving wildfire in Aude, southern France, after flames spread from Hérault and burned more than 800 hectares overnight.

stories.title: Any deal with Tehran is 'a pause before the next war,' Reza Pahlavi tells Euronews
stories.summary: In an exclusive interview with Euronews, Reza Pahlavi said the framework agreement would allow the Islamic Republic to regroup and rearm, with the only winner from the deal being the regime in Tehran.

stories.title: 11-year-old boy dies from rabies in Canada after waking up to a bat on his face
stories.summary: Rabies is a rare but deadly infection usually spread by a bite or scratch from an infected animal. It is almost always fatal after the onset of symptoms, although vaccination and early treatment can help to prevent it.

stories.title: Damascus explosion caused by bomb, state TV says, as death toll climbs to nine
stories.summary: The explosion comes a day after the first parliament since the fall of long-time leader Bashar al-Assad began to take shape.

stories.title: Cheers and applause as security guard rescued from rubble
stories.summary: Emergency workers managed to free Hernán Gil more than 100 hours after they had first located him under 140 tonnes of rubble.

stories.title: Iran renews Hormuz threats as Doha talks end with cautious progress
stories.summary: Tehran threatened a "forceful response" against vessels straying from its approved Hormuz routes, even as mediators said indirect US-Iran talks in Doha concluded with "positive progress". The next round of talks will wait until after Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's funeral, which begins on Saturday.

stories.title: French aerobatic team stages flypast over New York for US anniversary
stories.summary: Patrouille de France flies over New York with Alpha Jets and coloured smoke during celebrations marking the 250th anniversary of the US Declaration of Independence.

stories.title: UN to close its human rights office in Burkina Faso following junta's suspension
stories.summary: Junta chief captain Ibrahim Traoré has pursued anti-Western policies since seizing power in a coup in September 2022, while critical voices are met with repression.

stories.title: Venezuelan security guard pulled alive from basement eight days after twin quakes
stories.summary: The earthquakes destroyed tens of thousands of buildings across northern Venezuela, killing more than 2,200 people, and leaving La Guaira state as the hardest-hit region in the country.

stories.title: Daredevil couple charged with felonies for Empire State Building proposal stunt
stories.summary: The highest public floor is the 102nd, where there’s an observation deck. Going higher requires a key card, according to the court complaint.

stories.title: Former Olympian indicted on felony charge over alleged Reflecting Pool vandalism
stories.summary: District of Columbia US Attorney Jeanine Pirro said David Hearn ripped up recently installed sealant on pool in “a deliberate act” that caused more than €875 in damage.

stories.title: 6.2 earthquake hits eastern Indonesia
stories.summary: The tremor hit at a depth of 120 kilometres, roughly 58 kilometres west of Tobelo in North Maluku province, and officials confirmed there is no tsunami threat.

stories.title: Israelis mark 1,000 days since 7 October attack
stories.summary: Thousands gathered in Tel Aviv to mark 1,000 days since the 7 October 2023 attack, as former hostages and bereaved families addressed the crowd.

stories.title: Firefighters battle major wildfire in southern France
stories.summary: A major wildfire in France's Aude department burns 900 hectares as aircraft and firefighters battle the blaze and evacuees reach emergency shelters.

stories.title: Portugal fans celebrate World Cup win over Croatia
stories.summary: Thousands of Portugal fans gathered in central Lisbon to celebrate a 2-1 victory over Croatia, sending their team through to the World Cup last 16.

stories.title: Trump claims Iran has agreed to hold peace talks in Doha after recent clashes
stories.summary: US president posts that meeting will take place on Tuesday in Qatari capital after exchange of fire in strait of Hormuz Iran is jealously competing with Oman as decision-maker over strait of Hormuz Donald Trump has claimed that Iran has agreed to hold talks in Doha after the US and Tehran traded fire in the strait of Hormuz this weekend, threatening the collapse of a ceasefire meant to keep the strait open and pave the way for peace talks. In a terse post on Truth Social, the US president claime

stories.title: Ahead of NATO summit, Turkey arrests more than 200 activists
stories.summary: Protesters are clearly unwelcome ahead of the upcoming NATO summit in Ankara. Amid a strict public gatherings ban, 225 human rights and environmental activists as well as journalists have already been arrested.

stories.title: Russia's fuel crisis: Is Putin under pressure?
stories.summary: Ukraine's extended campaign against Russian energy infrastructure is disrupting fuel supplies and military logistics. With shortages mounting in occupied Crimea, is the pressure pushing Putin toward negotiations?

stories.title: Pakistani airstrikes kill dozens in eastern Afghanistan
stories.summary: Pakistan says strikes were aimed at a terrorist group while Taliban condemn ‘cowardly act of aggression’ Pakistani airstrikes in three eastern provinces of Afghanistan killed 36 civilians and wounded 163 others, Afghan officials have said, as attacks between the two countries showed no sign of abating. Pakistan’s information minister, Attaullah Tarar, said the operations on Sunday night were aimed at a terrorist group his country blamed for a deadly militant attack in Karachi that killed three s

stories.title: ‘Humanity is a privilege’: Umar Khalid on his six years in an Indian jail without trial
stories.summary: Exclusive: Activist tells of his life as one of India’s most prominent political prisoners and his opposition to the government of Narendra Modi Prison is hardest at sunset. As the thousands of prisoners incarcerated in Delhi’s most infamous jail are cast out of their cells and forced into the dank yard until darkness falls, prisoner number 626714 feels the punishing dread begin to rise. Yet the inmate – better known as Umar Khalid – was recently moved to discover that another political prisoner

stories.title: Delhi plans to ban petrol rickshaws and scooters in effort to cut toxic fumes
stories.summary: Government hopes for 30% of city’s fleet to be electric by 2030, in move hailed as ‘gamechanger’ on air pollution The unruly chaos of Delhi’s roads would be unrecognisable without the rickshaws and scooters that zip through India’s capital in their millions, emitting toxic fumes in their wake. But now, ambitious policies aim to give the city’s most recognisable vehicles an environmental makeover. On Monday, Delhi’s government announced plans to eventually ban petrol scooters, motorbikes and auto

stories.title: Pakistan roof collapse kills 14 children at tutoring centre
stories.summary: Local officials said preliminary reports showed the centre was unregistered and operating inside a privately owned residential building Fourteen children died after ⁠the roof of a tutoring centre collapsed in Pakistan’s eastern city of ⁠Lahore on Tuesday, ⁠rescue ​officials have said, as authorities opened the way for a possible negligence ⁠investigation. Punjab’s emergency service said rescuers found children and a 30-year-old female teacher ⁠under the rubble of the private after-school facilit

stories.title: Explosion in Monaco: Who is Vadym Yermolaiev?
stories.summary: Ukrainian-born businessman Vadym Yermolaiev was reportedly injured in a suspected assassination attempt in Monaco. Kyiv had previously imposed sanctions on him. What is known about the case?
